question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file with a `fileBufferSize`-sized header section (200,000 bytes of header data) that causes the `BufferedReader` to exhaust its buffer before reaching the actual balance data rows, causing `parseConsensusTimestamp()` to throw `InvalidDatasetException` and the entire file's balance records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `batch.queueCapacity = 1` setting to cause a race condition where a new balance file arrives while the queue is full, causing the file to be rejected without being parsed, and the mirror node to export stale balance data from the previous snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file with exactly `batchSize * 5` (1,000,000) entries matching the `batch.maxItems` threshold, causing the batch processor to treat the file as a complete batch and flush prematurely, while additional records in the file are silently dropped, resulting in missing balance records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `transactionTimeout = Duration.ofMinutes(5L)` setting by submitting a balance file with 200,000 accounts each having 200,000 token balances, causing the database transaction to time out mid-persist and roll back, resulting in all balance records for that file being missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StreamType.BALANCE` return value in `getStreamType()` to cause the `DateRangeCalculator.getFilter()` to apply an incorrect date range filter, where balance files with consensus timestamps at the boundary of the configured date range are incorrectly excluded, resulting in missing balance snapshots exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect balance records on the mirror node by exploiting the `frequency = Duration.ofSeconds(1L)` polling interval combined with the `processingTimeout` of 10 seconds, where a slow balance file parse causes the health check to fail and the parser to be restarted mid-transaction, resulting in partial balance data being committed to the database and exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file where account balance entries are ordered such that the `batchSize` boundary falls between an account balance and its associated token balances, causing the account balance to be persisted in one batch and the token balances in another, and if the second batch fails, the mirror node exports account balances without their corresponding token balances?

[File: 'File Name: 'importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Max` constraint on `batchSize` (only `@Min(1)` is enforced) to set `batchSize` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` via configuration, causing `new ArrayList<>(batchSize)` in `AccountBalanceFileParser.doParse()` to throw an `OutOfMemoryError`, which bypasses the retry mechanism (excluded from `@Retryable`) and causes all balance records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Max` constraint on `fileBufferSize` (only `@Min(1)` is enforced) to set `fileBufferSize` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` via configuration, causing `new BufferedReader(..., bufferSize)` in `CsvBalanceFileReader.read()` to allocate a 2GB buffer and trigger an `OutOfMemoryError`, causing all balance records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing balance records by exploiting the `retry.setMaxAttempts(3)` limit where a balance file with a corrupted last batch causes the first two batches to be persisted successfully but the third to fail, and after 3 retries the entire transaction is rolled back, resulting in the mirror node exporting no records from that file despite partial success?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `batch.maxFiles = 1` default (inherited from `AbstractParserProperties`) combined with `batch.queueCapacity = 1` to cause a scenario where only one balance file is ever processed per batch cycle, and if that file fails all 3 retry attempts, subsequent files in the queue are never processed, causing a permanent gap in mirror node balance exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file with a number of entries slightly above `batchSize` (e.g., 200,001) such that the last batch contains only 1 entry, and if the `batchPersister.persist()` for this single-entry batch fails within the `transactionTimeout`, the entire file's records are rolled back and missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Validated` annotation on `BalanceParserProperties` to cause a `ConstraintViolationException` during application startup by injecting an invalid configuration value (e.g., `batchSize=0`), preventing the balance parser from starting and causing all balance records to be permanently missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect balance records on the mirror node by exploiting the `batch.window = 5 minutes` setting, where balance files from two different consensus timestamps within the same 5-minute window are batched together, causing the parser to export a combined snapshot that incorrectly attributes balances from one timestamp to another?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file where the `fileBufferSize` (200,000 bytes) buffer boundary falls in the middle of a UTF-8 multi-byte character in an account identifier, causing `InputStreamReader` to produce a garbled character, which causes `AccountBalanceLineParser.parse()` to throw `InvalidDatasetException`, silently dropping that balance record and causing incorrect (missing) balance data on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `transactionTimeout = Duration.ofMinutes(5L)` by submitting a balance file that causes a database deadlock during `batchPersister.persist()`, which is not retried within the transaction but counts as one of the 3 `retry.setMaxAttempts(3)` attempts, causing the file to be abandoned after 3 deadlocks and its balance records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a balance file with `batchSize` (200,000) accounts all having the same token ID, causing the `tokenBalances` HashMap to have only 1 entry (due to `putIfAbsent` deduplication) while `accountBalances` has 200,000 entries, resulting in the token balance flush threshold never being reached and all token balances being persisted in a single final call that may exceed `transactionTimeout`, causing missing token balance records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing balance records by exploiting the interaction between `frequency = Duration.ofSeconds(1L)` and `processingTimeout` (10s default), where a balance file that takes more than 10 seconds to parse causes the health indicator to mark the parser as unhealthy, triggering an external restart that kills the in-progress transaction and causes the file's records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Component('balanceParserProperties')` bean name to cause a Spring bean conflict if another component registers with the same name, resulting in the wrong properties being injected into `AccountBalanceFileParser` and causing incorrect `batchSize` or `transactionTimeout` values that lead to missing or incorrect balance records on the mirror node?
